meta.source: FotMob-derived player export plus current team model
meta.squad_source_note: FIFA published final 48 squad lists on 2026-06-01. FotMob player-level ingestion still requires approved API access, a FotMob export, or verified player IDs.
meta.player_pool_rule: Top 410 World Cup players with at least 900 club minutes and 12 club appearances, ranked by impact score.

teams.profile: Elite ball progression and transition threat. The model likes Brazil most when the front line receives early against unsettled blocks.
High ceiling squad with strong duel profiles and tournament-tested shot creation. Defensive spacing is the key swing factor.
Control-heavy team with strong rest-defense and late-game management. Their path improves when match tempo stays low.
Deep attacking group with excellent penalty-box occupation. Chance quality is strong when midfield progression is clean.
Possession value and counterpressing keep their floor high. The model watches direct chance prevention against fast opponents.
Strong territory team with improving central combinations. Defensive transition efficiency determines the ceiling.
Technically rich attack with multiple ball-winners behind it. Shot selection and role balance remain the main model questions.
Reliable defensive structure and aerial value. Upside depends on converting controlled possession into central shots.
Aggressive pressing and verticality create upset equity. Card risk and game-state volatility add variance.
Attacking quality remains high, especially between the lines. The model is cautious about defensive recovery runs against elite pace.
Good ball-carrying and wide chance creation give Colombia real knockout equity. Set-piece defense is a key watch point.
Compact defensive phases and high-end transition runners make Morocco difficult to separate from in knockout matches.
Midfield control and tournament experience keep the floor stable. Chance volume will decide whether the ceiling is still elite.
Organized, mature, and comfortable in lower-event games. Switzerland project well when protecting narrow leads.
Athletic midfield pressure and home-region conditions give the USA useful upside. Final-third efficiency is the swing factor.
Mexico have host-region advantages and good midfield bite. The model wants more penalty-box shot quality against compact teams.
Clean technical tempo and coordinated pressure keep Japan dangerous against bigger names. Their transition defense rates well.
Physical, direct, and dangerous when recovering the ball high. Senegal's upside is strongest in open, duel-heavy matches.
Young, intense, and defensively assertive. Ecuador's model case improves when the midfield turns pressure into fast shots.
Austria bring structured pressing and sharp vertical attacks. The question is whether they can sustain chance quality deep into games.
Norway's star power gives them a dangerous attacking tail. Defensive block discipline shapes their knockout odds.
High-energy midfield play and brave possession make Turkiye volatile in a useful way. Defensive spacing is the downside risk.
Strong runners and set-piece presence create upset paths. The model likes their physical edge in late-game states.
Korea Republic combine disciplined shape with fast attacking outlets. Wide defensive matchups are the key variable.
Australia project as difficult to bully physically and useful on restarts. Open-play creation decides the ceiling.
Canada's speed in wide areas gives them clear transition upside. Central defensive control is the model watch point.
Sweden profile as organized and aerially strong, with useful direct attacking routes when games become stretched.
Scotland's intensity and wide service create pressure waves. The model needs more consistent shot quality to move them higher.
Czechia offer size, structure, and set-piece threat. Their upset probability rises in low-scoring knockout scripts.
Paraguay's defensive aggression and restart value keep them live. Sustained possession against top teams is the concern.
Egypt combine veteran attacking quality with compact defensive phases. Chance prevention after turnovers is critical.
Ghana have ball-carrying and athletic midfield tools. Their model path depends on reducing defensive volatility.
Saudi Arabia's coordinated pressing and tournament experience create tricky group-stage matchups.
IR Iran are strong in compact phases and difficult to break down. Their attacking efficiency is highly game-state sensitive.
Qatar project best in controlled-possession matches. Defensive recovery pace is the main issue against elite transition teams.
South Africa bring organized pressing and useful midfield running. Shot generation against settled blocks is the model concern.
Tunisia's defensive discipline makes them awkward in low-event matches. Progression through midfield is the swing factor.
Algeria have technical midfielders and strong attacking flashes. The model needs cleaner defensive control.
Bosnia and Herzegovina bring experienced profiles and set-piece danger. Their ceiling depends on transition defense.
Uzbekistan's organization and improving ball progression make them a credible first-tournament problem.
Iraq's long qualifying path points to resilience and game management. Chance creation volume is the key model limit.
Jordan enter with momentum and direct attacking routes. Defensive box protection shapes their upset probability.
New Zealand rate well in aerial and set-piece phases. Open-play chance creation is the main hurdle.
Panama are organized and comfortable in physical matches. Their model path needs efficient finishing.
Congo DR carry athletic upside and transition danger. The model flags defensive spacing between lines.
Cabo Verde project as compact and difficult to rush. Shot volume against higher-rated opponents is the key challenge.
Haiti's best path comes through speed, transition moments, and disciplined defending during long spells without the ball.
Curacao arrive with historic momentum and useful technical profiles. The model treats them as high-variance underdogs.

matches.analysis: Brazil own the higher attacking ceiling, but the Netherlands reduce open-play chaos well. First goal probability strongly changes the live forecast.
France project as favorites on shot quality, while Uruguay's press creates the main upset path through turnovers in the middle third.
A near coin-flip after regulation. Argentina gain control value; England gain box threat and set-piece leverage.
